name: migration
description: Stage-first large-porting planner: inventory contracts/dependencies/tests, expand migration items, and reduce into ordered batches for the runtime task.

tasks.prompt: Use the runtime task to inventory user-visible contracts that must survive the port: CLIs, commands, APIs, schemas, file formats, config, error behavior, and compatibility expectations. Return JSON only with migrationItems, an array of concrete item objects. Each item must include id, title, area, sourcePaths, targetPaths, dependencies, risk, and validationHints. Include only repository-evidenced items.

Use the runtime task to inventory implementation modules that need porting or adaptation: core runtime paths, adapters, integrations, state transitions, and important internal boundaries. Return JSON only with migrationItems, an array of concrete item objects with id, title, area, sourcePaths, targetPaths, dependencies, risk, and validationHints. Prefer independently reviewable slices.

Use the runtime task to inventory state, persistence, generated artifacts, caches, lock files, migrations, and data compatibility concerns. Return JSON only with migrationItems, an array of concrete item objects with id, title, area, sourcePaths, targetPaths, dependencies, risk, and validationHints. Flag ordering constraints explicitly in dependencies.

Use the runtime task to inventory tests, fixtures, CI/local tooling, commands, package scripts, and validation gates that must be updated or preserved. Return JSON only with migrationItems, an array of concrete item objects with id, title, area, sourcePaths, targetPaths, dependencies, risk, and validationHints. Prefer validation-first slices and flag checks that depend on sensitive work such as dependency install, broad edits, long or expensive validation, or package/audit commands.

each.prompt: Create a bounded porting plan for this migration item: ${item}. Return JSON only with item, plan, dependencies, risks, validation, rollback, and recommendedBatch. The plan must be small enough for one implementation-slice batch or explicitly explain how to split it. Include exact files or directories to inspect first and concrete parity checks.

stages.prompt: Use Source Stage Context and the runtime task to synthesize a staged large-porting plan. Return JSON only with phases, workItems, validation, risks, rollback, and openDecisions. If acceptance needs sensitive work such as dependency install, broad edits, long or expensive validation, or package/audit commands and the runtime task did not explicitly allow it, list that as an openDecision instead of assuming permission. Order phases by dependency and risk: discovery/parity baselines first, compatibility shims before broad rewrites, tests before behavior changes where possible, and high-risk cutovers last. Each work item must be independently reviewable and suitable for a later implementation-slice run. Do not propose automatic merge or deployment.

catalog.mutationRisk: planning read-only unless child implementation is generated later
